History of the Di`Zok  
 

Lore Item Quest Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
AC: 15
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
CHA: +2 WIS: +2 INT: +2 HP: +35 MANA: +35
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: CLR DRU SHM NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

Perhaps for a quest?
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2001 at 4:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757464726
261 posts
I'm looking at this and am seriously wondering if it's for a future quest. As a cleric I've ALWAYS had some type of wisdow item in my main hand. In Kith a +6 wis mace drops. It's free and a single group or less can kill the mobs that drop it.

This item, while 15AC, is for the most part difficult to obtain for the overall low stat bonuses.

Personally, I would put it in the bank and hope it's for a quest. It's pretty useless as a mainhand item.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Nov 19 2001 at 2:52 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768229189
40 posts
While it's not much of a wis caster item, it's nice for int casters. Not many 15ac hand slot items, (primary hand?) and 200 int is trivial.

Too bad this isn't range =o)

Hell... I'm using a damn Crude Stein right now.. I'd use this if it dropped for my group and I won the roll.
▲ Page top
RE: Perhaps for a quest?
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2001 at 10:33 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755753632
33 posts
I presume you are referring to this mace:
Ruined Battleworn Morning Star
Type, Slot Weapon, 1HB
Damage DMG: 7 DMG Bonus: 0 DLY: 33 DPS: 4.55
Boosts +6 WIS, -5 DR

And if you're trying to tell me that that is better than this you have seriously fell off something and landed on your head. The wisdom on this item is completely trivial anyway. If you are having trouble capping out your wisdom then get an enchanter buff. There are plenty of other alternatives that are easy to get with plenty of wis on them. The beauty of this item is the fact that not only does it have 15AC on it but also 35hp and 35mana. If you don't understand the significance or importance of such a stat I really have to wonder how you got to being a "High Priest" to begin with unless you were picked up ebayed. Wis is ridiculously easy to cap to begin with making +HP and +Mana items king in the end to go hand and hand with resist gear.

Only reason I wouldn't be using this item in either hand is that I'm using a Sprinkler of the Spirits and an OotIV. I would DEFINITELY shelve my Crystal Chitin Shield back in the old days for this item, however.
▲ Page top
RE: Perhaps for a quest?
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2001 at 3:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757464726
261 posts
True to form, someone has a difficult time disagreeing without being a jackass.

If you think I'm ebay, how about you log onto povar tonite and send me a tell. I'll be on Apallohadas, Tough Guy. If it'll make you feel better I have my epic in one hand and a Shield of the Elders in the other. Perhaps between hits on the crack pipe you could look up the stats.

On to why I mentioned the RBMS. Look at where it drops if you can take 5 minutes from the full time job of being a jerk. Kith Forest at night. Often they simply rot. Look at where this SUBPAR item drops. Chardok. A zone frequented by 50+ characters and guilds on raids.

A low level cleric can easily get a RMBS on their own ability by walking over there and hanging out.

In the future, try to REALLY be a Big Tough Guy and debate instead of being a prick.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Nov 07 2001 at 7:04 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) No, the other guy is right. If you're level 60 and whining about 200 wisdom, then, you are not fulfilling the role of playing a cleric very well at all.

▲ Page top
RE: Perhaps for a quest?
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2001 at 12:34 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757464726
261 posts
Hail Moron!

WTF are you babbling on about? Please share with the rest of the class.

1) If you have a disc, you're obviously not a cleric. Twit.
2) What does ANY of this have to do with either the item in question, where it drops, or resistances? Did you look at the item or did you just feel like spewling your lunch randomly?
3) What does this have to do with the GM events for Halloween? I really wonder why you bothered to share your tale of glory with us.
4) Please quote where I whined about 200 wisdom. If you feel better, my wisdom is 225 and drops to 205 in resistance armor. My AC is still over 1K even with BD gear replacing ears, bracers and rings.
5) Your own words here: "My heat resistance, when buffed, has hit 198, but I've never fought and large encounter that is fire based (nagafen, for example) so can't comment on that yet."

WHAT ARE YOU BABBLING ABOUT?

Do us all a favor, look at the item and what's been said next time. Then if your mother lets you stay up past 6pm, try to engage in a discussion instead of the obvious pissing contest you've engaged in your own little mind.

What's wrong with some of you that you don't know how to DISCUSS without being a moron?

I'm done with this since neither you or the other flamer bothered to log onto povar and talk to me. I'll be on longer this weekend if nothing interesting is up and you want to send hate messages to my ignore filter.

# Nov 05 2001 at 7:30 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
lot of items in gam aren't obtainable at the level that would most likely get the most use out of em. is quite likely that it is an item to give to lower guildies or sell, or possibly give to someone who had weak itme in that slot already. lot of ppl at 50 have WAY more than 200 int/wis, but many just want cool item, or, as you mentioned, more HP. then again, is quite possible that this item is meant to give/sell to lower players. many items are merely to sell to merchants so ... why not?

thus i fail to your point.

talk to the librarian in the SK guildhouse in Cabilis...
▲ Page top
RE: lot of items in game...
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2001 at 4:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756514767
Scholar
19 posts
This book is not needed by the librarian in the Shadow Knight guild in East Cabilis as far as I know. Another book drops off Loremaster Piza`Tak in Chardok that is called "The History of Greenmist" (whether or not you cast Identify on it), and is to be given to the librarian as the first part of the quest for Greenmist. However, who knows? Maybe the librarian might give out more than one quest and this book is used. /shrug

Predatorus Viktus
"The Father," First Order of Blood Knights
52nd Scale Reaver, Shadow Division
Bristlebane Server

"There is an ancient Iksar saying, that something lasts only as long as the last person remembers it. My people have come to trust memory over history. Memory, like fire, is radiant and immutable. Those who renounce the flame of memory in order to put out the dangerous fire of truth: Beware these men, for they are dangerous themselves... and unwise. There are false histories written on the blood of those who might remember, and on those who seek the truth."
▲ Page top
RE: lot of items in game...
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2001 at 9:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755048933
Scholar
127 posts
Once any caster starts to get over 200 Int or Wis, the focus on upgrades for them becomes AC, Resists, and Mana, to varying degrees. By the time any caster gets this item, they will most likely have several High stat increase items boosting their Int/Wis, like GEBS or an Emerald Dragonscale BP (for wis casters). With that in mind, the AC, HP, and Mana on this are NICE. Sure it has less Wis than say, a Testament of Vanear, but that 7 or 8 less Wis translates to under 20 mana if the stat is over 200. I can see players using this easily in the 50-60 range, myself.
▲ Page top
RE: lot of items in game...

Anyone else notice the AC:15 part?

# Jan 12 2003 at 4:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362297633
37 posts
well we raided tonight and I passed on the range item that had 10 int and other stats so i could roll on this. I like the ac and the hp and mana for primary I use a drachnid thyxyl in secondary which has 50 mana 5 int and mr so being lvl 56 and over 200 int anyway this is a nice item to hold for now. Easy to get range items and secondary but not a primary slash secondary with ac btw my secondary has ac forgot amount on it but it all helps at this lvl.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 05 2001 at 7:00 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i know this a a really nice item, but let's be realistic fo a moment, Clerics? i think not especially at this lvl, I mean a lvl 50 better have something better than this (unless they have capped their wis and want some mana ac and hp incase of aggro) Dru. seriously doubt and lvl 50 druid would want this, shm same deal, Nec, Wiz Mag lol lol hahahahahaha! don't think so, enchanter well that's kinda the same deal as cleric. The only reason that i see a class at lvl 50 would want this, is A) plane simple stupididty (ironic how i spell isn't it?) B) mana hp and ac, but let's get back to REAL life, and say this a a twink item. Feel free to flame this is just my oppinion
